impact: Runtime validation currently reads full MDI, Stripe, and app-signing payloads from server-only JSON environment variables. That is an interim bridge until a runtime AWS Secrets Manager client can fetch values by secret identifier with least-privilege IAM, avoiding duplicated live credentials in hosting env configuration.
resolution: Added an AWS Secrets Manager runtime startup source that fetches required secret payloads by stage-scoped secret identifiers, changed startup validation to require secret IDs/ARNs by default instead of full JSON env payloads, kept JSON env payloads behind an explicit local/test fallback flag, wired the Stripe webhook route through the runtime secret source, and documented the production identifier variables.
title: Runtime secret loading still uses interim JSON env bindings
